modules.title: Water – Analysis, Treatments and Industrial Applications
topics: Determination of hardness by EDTA method
Alkalinity determination and related numerical problems

modules.title: Polymer & Polymerisation
topics: Properties and uses of Nylon 6 and Nylon 6:6
Properties and uses of Buna N and Buna S

modules.title: Phase Equilibrium and Corrosion
topics: Phase diagram of binary eutectic system (Cu‑Ag)

experiments: Water testing – determination of total hardness by complexometric titration; determination of mixed alkalinity (OH & CO₃ and CO₃ & HCO₃); chloride ion estimation by argentometric method.

Fuels & lubricant testing – flash and fire points determination using Pensky‑Martin, Abel’s and Cleveland’s open‑cup apparatus; calorific value determination by bomb calorimeter; viscosity and viscosity‑index determination using Redwood viscometers No.1 and No.2; proximate analysis of coal (moisture, ash, volatile‑matter, carbon‑residue); steam‑emulsification number and aniline‑point determination; cloud‑ and pour‑point determination of lubricating oil.

Alloy analysis – percentage of Fe in iron alloy by redox titration using N‑Phenyl anthranilic acid as internal indicator; determination of Cu and/or Cr in alloy by iodometric titration; % purity determination of ferrous ammonium sulfate and copper sulfate.

modules.title: DC Circuits
topics: Voltage and current sources (independent and dependent)
Power and energy calculations in resistive circuits

modules.title: 1‑Phase AC Circuits
topics: Average, RMS values, form factor and peak factor
Analysis of R‑L, R‑C, R‑L‑C series and parallel circuits
Three‑phase AC fundamentals: phase sequence, balanced/unbalanced supply and loads
Relationship between line and phase quantities for star and delta connections
Power measurement in balanced and unbalanced three‑phase systems

modules.title: Magnetic Circuits
topics: Energy storage in linear magnetic systems
Magnetic field produced by current‑carrying conductors
Induced voltage and laws of electromagnetic induction

modules.title: Electrical Machines
topics: Construction, classification and working principles of DC machines
Construction, classification and working principles of induction machines
Construction, classification and working principles of synchronous machines
Slip concept in three‑phase induction motors
Types of losses in electrical machines
Applications of DC, induction and synchronous machines

modules.title: Basic Electronics
topics: Number systems and conversions used in digital electronics
Logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR)
Bipolar junction transistor (BJT) operation and configurations (CE, CB, CC)

experiments: Basic safety precautions. Introduction and use of measuring instruments – voltmeter, ammeter, multi-meter, oscilloscope. Real‑life resistors, capacitors and inductors.

Measuring the steady‑state and transient time‑response of R‑L, R‑C, and R‑L‑C circuits to a step change in voltage (transient observed on a storage oscilloscope). Sinusoidal steady‑state response of R‑L and R‑C circuits – impedance calculation and verification. Observation of phase differences between current and voltage. Resonance in R‑L‑C circuits.

Transformers: Observation of the no‑load current waveform on an oscilloscope (non‑sinusoidal wave‑shape due to B‑H curve non‑linearity) with discussion of harmonics. Loading of a transformer: measurement of primary and secondary voltages, currents and power.

Determination of equivalent circuit parameters of a single‑phase transformer by open‑circuit and short‑circuit tests; estimation of voltage regulation and efficiency at various loading conditions and verification by load test.

Demonstration of cut‑out sections of machines: DC machine (commutator‑brush arrangement), induction machine (squirrel‑cage rotor), synchronous machine (field winding‑slip‑ring arrangement) and single‑phase induction machine.
Torque‑speed characteristic of a separately excited DC motor.

Synchronous speed of two‑ and four‑pole, three‑phase induction motors. Direction reversal by changing phase‑sequence. Torque‑slip characteristic of an induction motor. Generator operation of an induction machine driven at super‑synchronous speed.
Synchronous machine operating as a generator: stand‑alone operation with a load and control of voltage through field excitation.
Study of V‑I characteristics of diodes.
Applications of diodes and their verification.
Transistor applications as amplifier and switch.
Verification of truth tables for various gates and flip‑flops.
Realisation of various gates and flip‑flops.
Verification of De Morgan’s theorems.
reference_books: D.P. Kothari & I.J. Nagrath, Basic Electrical Engineering, Tata McGraw Hill, latest edition.
S.N. Singh, Basic Electrical Engineering, P.H.I., 2013.

B.L. Theraja & A.K. Theraja, Textbook of Electrical Technology – Vol. 1, S. Chand Publication.
E. Hughes & I.M. Smith, Hughes Electrical Technology, Pearson.
Vincent Del Toro, Electrical Engineering Fundamentals.

experiments: To determine the dispersive power of prism.
To determine the wavelength $\lambda$ of sodium light with the help of Newton’s ring.
Resolving power of telescope.
Young’s double slit experiment (YDSE).
To determine the frequency of AC mains supply.
$V\!\!-\!I$ characteristics of $p\!\-\!n$ junction diode.
To determine the wavelength $\lambda$ of diode lines by single slit diffraction.
To determine Planck’s constant with the help of photocell.
Hall’s effect experiment.
Calibration of ammeter using reference Zener diode.
Study the effect of temperature on reverse saturation current in $p\!\-\!n$ junction diode and determine the energy band gap.
To determine the wavelength $\lambda$ of sodium using plane diffraction grating.
To determine the prominent lines of mercury source using plane diffraction grating.
To determine the numerical aperture of an optical fiber.
To determine wavelength $\lambda$ of given laser using plane diffraction grating.

modules.title: Computer Fundamentals and Operating System
topics: Computer definition, classification, and organization (CPU, registers, bus architecture, instruction set, memory & storage systems, I/O devices, system & application software)
